info.title: Greenhouse Platform — Operator/Fleet REST API
info.description: The platform Go API's operator-facing REST surface (catalog contract #4) — the request/response half of the contract the React SPA (and operator tooling) consumes (frontend data model §1; platform interfaces §3). It covers the fleet registry, per-greenhouse detail, ad-hoc setpoint edits, historical telemetry range queries, and aggregated analytics (slice 2a), plus crop-profile CRUD and assignments (slice 2b). Each operation is tagged with `x-slice`. Live push (telemetry, status, drift, events) is a separate WebSocket contract and is not described here. Identity, units, and timestamps follow RFC-007 — the same `greenhouse_id` / `zone_id` slugs that key MQTT topics, controller REST paths, and DB rows; field names are snake_case to match the MQTT and controller-rest contracts (the SPA maps them to its camelCase types). Authentication is slice-dependent: 2a is unauthenticated on the trusted local Docker network (RFC-011, superseding RFC-009) — its operations declare `security: []`; 2b operations require a Keycloak OIDC bearer token (`bearerAuth`), with writes restricted to the operator role (platform security §4). A rejected write returns 422 with a body naming the violated field and bound, mirroring the controller REST contract. `info.version` is the contract version; its major bumps on a breaking change, recorded in an ADR (RFC-007). This document is split: each path is a file under `paths/`, and shared schemas, parameters, and responses live under `components/`; run `redocly bundle` to emit a single self-contained file.

servers.description: The platform's nginx entry point, which serves the SPA and reverse-proxies `/api` to the Go API. The browser never reaches the Go API, MQTT, or controllers directly.

bearerAuth.description: (2b) Keycloak-issued OIDC access token. Validated by the Go API (signature, issuer, audience, expiry); its roles map onto the platform's viewer/operator roles (platform security). Not used in 2a, which is unauthenticated on the trusted Docker network (RFC-011, superseding RFC-009).
